notes: Jupiter swap routes transfer-fee tokens; the fee is deducted from the received amount and quotes account for it.
Transfer-tax tokens are not supported on Limit orders — creators can change the fee rate at will, causing unexpected execution.
Same restriction as Limit orders: transfer-tax tokens are not supported on Recurring/DCA.
Jupiter swap can route transfer-hook mints by appending hook accounts to the instruction, but support is limited to hooks Jupiter can resolve; unresolvable hook programs cause routes to be excluded.
Non-transferable (soulbound) tokens block all transfers, so swaps are impossible by design.
Mechanically transferable so swaps route, but a permanent delegate can drain accounts; flagged as a high-risk extension.
If accounts default to frozen, transfers fail unless the freeze authority thaws them; tradability is conditional.
Requires an incoming memo on transfers; routing must include the memo or transfers to the account fail.

The extension does not block normal (public) transfers, so the mint can still be swapped via the standard path; confidential balances themselves are not routable. AMM routing operates on public balances only.
Sub-extension of confidential transfer; inherits the same public-balance-only routing caveat.
Display-only UI scaling; does not affect transfer mechanics, so it routes normally.
Display-only UI scaling of amounts; underlying raw amounts are unchanged, so it routes normally.
Display/metadata-only; does not affect transfers. Jupiter reads it for token info.
On-chain metadata extension; display-only, no transfer impact.
Informational grouping pointer; does not block transfers.
Informational grouping data; does not block transfers.
Informational group-member pointer; does not block transfers.
Informational group-member data; does not block transfers.
Allows the mint to be closed and reinitialized at the same address — a known risk vector; transfers work while the mint is live but it is flagged as risky.
When the mint is paused, all transfers fail and swaps revert; tradability depends on the pause state controlled by the authority.
Account-level extension that does not block standard transfers; treated as non-blocking.
Integrators are advised to use an extension allowlist; unrecognized extensions are conservatively excluded from routing.
Jupiter officially supports Token-2022 on Instant Swap. Scope matters: transfer-fee tokens are supported on swap but explicitly NOT on Limit orders or Recurring/DCA.
Verdicts combine Jupiter's own docs/support pages with Token-2022 mechanics (Neodyme, Solana docs); display-only extensions route normally, transfer-halting extensions block or condition swaps.
